goal: Prepare WhatIfInvest for first App Store review by adding a production app icon, matching launch screen treatment, App Store Connect automation scaffolding, and a verified archive path.
why_now: The app has an empty AppIcon set and placeholder launch mark, and needs the same deployment-readiness path used by Hi_Tomorrow before first review.
in_scope: Generate a What If Invest app icon aligned with the app's blue-and-white beginner fintech identity
Populate the iOS app icon asset catalog with required iPhone and marketing icon PNGs
Update LaunchScreen.storyboard to show the brand icon instead of the placeholder square
Add Hi_Tomorrow-style ASC helper scripts, env template, metadata drafts, and documentation
Run build/archive verification and attempt App Store Connect id discovery after archive/setup
non_goals: Submitting the app for review without explicit final confirmation
Changing product functionality or investor simulation behavior
Adding paid features, ads, subscriptions, or analytics
Creating official stock or ETF logos
Hardcoding private App Store Connect credentials into the repository

decision_boundaries.decision: Choose the final icon visual direction within the approved blue fintech hindsight-investing motif
Create ASC scripts and metadata scaffold with empty or discovered ids
decision_boundaries.notes: User requested adding this even though this is first review.
The requested scope is archive and review readiness, not final submission.

decision_boundaries.decision: Store API keys or private credentials
decision_boundaries.notes: Automation may read local env files but must not commit secrets.

constraints: Follow WhatIfInvest DESIGN.md: trust-first, clean blue-and-white fintech, not a trading-terminal look
Keep English and Korean App Store metadata drafts together
Reuse Hi_Tomorrow automation shape where practical
Support first-review setup where APP_ID, APP_INFO_ID, and VERSION_ID may initially be unknown
Do not revert unrelated parent KinnoLabs gitlink state

acceptance_criteria.description: AppIcon.appiconset contains generated PNG assets including a 1024x1024 marketing icon and valid Contents.json entries.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Asset catalog inspection and Xcode build

acceptance_criteria.description: LaunchScreen.storyboard shows the new brand icon and existing localized product copy on a matching light fintech background.
WhatIfInvest includes ASC scripts, env template, metadata drafts, and README guidance adapted from Hi_Tomorrow.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Script review and shell syntax/readiness checks

acceptance_criteria.description: The app can be archived with the WhatIfInvest scheme or a concrete blocker is reported.
ASC env values are filled where discoverable, or the remaining first-review setup gap is explicit.
acceptance_criteria.verification: asc discovery command output and .asc/env contents
